name: Tanioka's Seafoods
tagline: West-side pilgrimage spot — the smoked meat counter alone is worth the drive from town.
mustOrder: ahi patties ($3–4 each, crispy outside, tender inside — tourists overlook these)
talkStory: The Tanioka family has been rooted in Waipahu's plantation community since the 1970s, and Grandma Tanioka's original recipes still form the foundation. Lines go out the door during lunch rush and popular items sell out by mid-afternoon. Their poke counter is elite, but the sleeper hits are the ahi patties ($3-4 each, crispy outside, tender inside) and the smoked marlin that tourists walk right past. You can taste before buying a pound. Bring a cooler if you're driving back to town — the smoked meats are incredible take-home.
